text: The limit of resolution of an oil immersion objective microscope of numerical aperture 0.8 for light of wavelength 0.6 µm is
explanation:
Numerical aperture, \( NA = 0.8 \)

Wavelength, \( \lambda = 0.6 \) µm

Limit of resolution,

\( \frac{\lambda}{2 NA} = \frac{0.6}{2 \times 0.8} = \frac{0.3}{0.8} = \frac{3}{8} \) µm.
name: Questions Item 1

text: Light from a source travels out with a velocity \( c \). If the source moves away from the observer with a velocity \( v \), then the relative velocity of light with respect to observer is
explanation: The speed of light in vacuum is \( c \) for any inertial reference. This is true no matter how fast a light source is moving relative to an observer.
name: Questions Item 2

text: \( PQ \) represents a wavefront and \( AO \) and \( BP \), the corresponding two rays. Find the condition on \( Q \) for constructive interference at \( P \) between ray \( BP \) and reflected ray \( OP \).
explanation:
Path difference \( \Delta = OP + OQ = OP(1 + \cos 2\theta) = 2d \cos \theta \).

For constructive interference, \( \Delta = \frac{\lambda}{2} \).

\( \therefore 2d \cos \theta = \frac{\lambda}{2} \Rightarrow \cos \theta = \frac{\lambda}{4d} \).
name: Questions Item 3

text: A Fraunhofer diffraction pattern due to a narrow slit is obtained on a screen placed at a distance \( D \) from the slit whose slit width is \( a \). The distance of first secondary maximum from the central maximum is
explanation:
Direction of secondary maximum \( \theta = (2n + 1) \frac{\lambda}{2a} \) (\( n=1 \)).

\( \theta = \frac{3\lambda}{2a} \).

Distance \( x = \theta D = \frac{3D\lambda}{2a} \).
name: Questions Item 4

text: The central fringe in the interference pattern obtained in Young's double slit experiment will be a dark fringe when the phase difference between the waves from the two slits is
explanation:
For central dark fringe, path difference \( \Delta x = \frac{\lambda}{2} \).

Phase difference \( \Delta \phi = \frac{2\pi}{\lambda} \times \frac{\lambda}{2} = \pi \).
name: Questions Item 5

text: Which of the following generates a plane wavefront ?
explanation: An extended source produces plane wavefront. A point source produces spherical wavefront.
name: Questions Item 6

text: Two-point white dots are 2mm apart on a black paper. They are viewed by eye of pupil diameter 3 mm. What is the maximum distance at which these dots can be resolved by the eye? (\( \lambda = 500 \) nm)
name: Questions Item 7

explanation:
For diffraction, width \( a \) of slit must be less than or comparable to wavelength.

\( \frac{a}{\lambda} \le 1 \).
name: Questions Item 8

text: A mixture of yellow light of wavelength 580 nm and blue light of wavelength 450 nm is incident normally on an air film of thickness \( 2.9 \times 10^{-4} \) mm. The colour of reflected light is
explanation:
For air film, \( 2t = (2m+1) \frac{\lambda}{2} \Rightarrow \lambda = \frac{4t}{2m+1} \).

Closest to 450 nm is blue.
name: Questions Item 9

text: In a Young's double slit experiment, if the slit separation is twice the wavelength of light used, then the maximum number of interference maxima is
explanation:
\( d \sin \theta = n \lambda \Rightarrow n = 2 \sin \theta \).

Maximum \( n = 2 \). Possible integers: -2,-1,0,1,2 → 5 maxima.
name: Questions Item 10

text: The angular deviation of 5th order dark fringe is 12° in a single slit experiment. If the width of the slit is 9 µm then the wavelength of the incident light is
name: Questions Item 11

text: Unpolarised light from air incidents on the surface of a transparent medium of refractive index 1.414 such that the reflected light is completely polarised. Match the angles given in List-I with the corresponding values given in List-II.
explanation:
Polarising angle \( i_p = \sin^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2}{3}} \).

Refraction angle \( r = \sin^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \).

Deviation \( \delta = \sin^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2}{3}} - \sin^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \).

Angle of reflection \( \cos^{-1} \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \).

Angle between incident and polarised \( 2 \sin^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{2}{3}} \).
name: Questions Item 12

text: A Young's double slit experimental setup is immersed in water of refractive index 1.33. It has slit separation 1 mm and the distance between slits and screen is 1.33 m. If the wavelength of incident light on slits is 6300 Å, then the fringe width on the screen is
name: Questions Item 13

text: A ray of light is incident on the surface of a glass plate of refractive index \( \sqrt{3} \) at the polarising angle. The angle of refraction of the ray is
name: Questions Item 14

text: In a Young's double slit experiment, the two slits are separated by 0.5 cm and the screen is at 0.5 m from the slits. If 20000 bright fringes are counted per meter on the screen, then the wavelength of light used is
name: Questions Item 15

text: In a Young's double slit experiment, light of wavelength 5900 Å is used. When the slits are 2 mm apart, the fringe width is 1.2 mm. If the slit separation is increased to one and half times the previous value, then the fringe width will be
name: Questions Item 16

text: Two polaroids are placed in the path of unpolarised light beam of intensity \( I_0 \) such that no light is emitted from the second polaroid. If a third polaroid whose polarisation axis makes an angle \( \theta \) with that of the first polaroid is placed between the polaroids, then intensity of light emerging from the last polaroid is
name: Questions Item 17

text: A microscope has an objective of aperture 8 mm and focal length of 5 cm. The minimum separation between two objects to be just resolved by the microscope is (wavelength of light used = 5500 Å)
name: Questions Item 18

text: Two point sources \( S_1 \) and \( S_2 \) separated by a distance 10 μm emit light waves of wavelength 4 μm in phase. A circular wire of radius 40 μm is placed around the sources as shown in figure, then (O is the centre of the circle and \( OS_1 = OS_2 \))
options: points A and B are dark and points C and D are bright
points A and B are bright and points C and D are dark
points A and C are dark and points B and D are bright
points A and C are bright and points B and D are dark
explanation: Path difference at A and C: constructive (bright), at B and D: destructive (dark).
name: Questions Item 19
